title: Simple Subquery
description: Use a query nested inside a WHERE clause to filter results based on another query's output.
problemStatement:
Queries within queries! Find all `products` whose `price` is greater than the average price of all products.

**Requirement:** Use a subquery `(SELECT AVG(price) FROM products)` inside your WHERE clause.
hints: Subqueries allow you to perform dynamic filtering based on aggregated data.
expectedOutput: List of products that are more expensive than average
solutionCode: SELECT * FROM products WHERE price > (SELECT AVG(price) FROM products);
